248.reason: [When encountering a single '&', the code consumes the next character unconditionally via `line.charAt(idx++)`, which may cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException if '&' is the last character on the line. Additionally, even if safe, it fails to properly backtrack or handle the consumed character, leading to skipped tokens or incorrect error reporting.]

261.reason: [Same issue as above: `line.charAt(idx++)` is called without checking if `idx < line.length()`, risking an IndexOutOfBoundsException when '|' is the last character. Also, the consumed character after '|' is not processed further, potentially skipping valid tokens or misreporting errors.]
